decks.notes: This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts. It utilizes cards like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb to ramp into threats like Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The inclusion of key artifacts such as Time Vault, The One Ring, and Talon Gates of Madara provide additional win conditions and utility. The deck also features disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on fast, aggressive plays with a strong artifact theme.

This Esper Control deck focuses on leveraging powerful creatures and disruptive spells to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It includes powerful card advantage engines like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time, as well as efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also includes countermagic like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect its own game plan. With Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion, the deck can continuously recur key threats and disrupt the opponent's strategies, making it a challenging and versatile control deck to face.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Oath of Druids card to cheat powerful creatures into play quickly, supported by counterspells and disruption to control the game. Key cards like Ancestral Recall, Show and Tell, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier can help establish board presence while cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will help disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes versatile tutors like Demonic Tutor and Vampiric Tutor to search for key pieces, creating a strong and adaptable strategy to dominate the battlefield.

The deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier, while controlling the game with counterspells like Force of Will and disruptive cards like Thoughtseize. It also includes alternative win conditions through Show and Tell and Oko, Thief of Crowns. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation spells to ensure consistency in drawing key pieces and disrupting the opponent's game plan. Additionally, it includes mana acceleration in the form of Moxen and ramp lands to cast powerful spells ahead of schedule.

The deck relies on fast mana acceleration and artifact synergy to pull off a powerful combo involving Coveted Jewel. The key strategy is to generate a large amount of mana quickly using cards like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith, then use cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Mystic Forge to draw through the deck and find Coveted Jewel. Once Coveted Jewel is on the battlefield, the opponent will have a hard time keeping up with the card advantage and mana advantage provided by its ability. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will to protect the combo.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage, while also utilizing the Gifts Ungiven package to search for key combo pieces. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win conditions, such as Brain Freeze or Flame of Anor. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value to be gained from the graveyard, providing extra resources and resilience in longer games. With a strong mana base and powerful spells, this deck looks to outmaneuver and outlast the opponent in a Vintage 1 vs 1 match.

The deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers. It also includes disruptive elements like Null Rod and Mindbreak Trap to control the board and protect the combo. The deck aims to control the game through counterspells like Force of Will and disruption spells like Thoughtseize, while also having the option to ramp up with Black Lotus and other fast mana sources. Overall, the deck aims to win through a combination of powerful creatures and control elements.

The deck focuses on utilizing the powerful card advantage and mana acceleration provided by cards like Paradoxical Outcome, Moxen, and Tolarian Academy to generate a large amount of mana and draw cards. By combining these resources with powerful win conditions like Thundertrap Trainer or Hexing Squelcher, the deck aims to efficiently assemble a game-winning combo or overwhelming board state. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and redundancy to the deck's strategy, ensuring consistency and resilience against disruption.

The deck revolves around utilizing artifacts and card draw to generate a large amount of mana and draw cards, ultimately aiming to assemble a winning combination of cards to achieve victory. Key cards include Paradoxical Outcome, Transmute Artifact, and Tinker, paired with powerful artifacts like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Misdirection to protect its combo pieces. The goal is to efficiently draw through the deck, generate mana, and assemble the necessary combo pieces to win the game quickly.

The main strategy of this deck is to use the card Doomsday to set up a winning combo with Thassa's Oracle. By carefully manipulating your deck and resources, you can craft a sequence of cards that will allow you to win the game in one explosive turn. The deck includes various tutors, card draw spells, and counter magic to help you assemble the combo efficiently and protect it from disruption. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a valuable companion that can be cast from the sideboard to provide additional support and flexibility.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying a variety of artifacts to disrupt the opponent's game plan while ramping up mana with lands like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb. Cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge provide card advantage and utility, while Golos, Tireless Pilgrim helps to search for key pieces. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of disruptive artifacts, powerful mana acceleration, and threats like Lodestone Golem and Stonecoil Serpent, ultimately setting up for a quick victory through aggressive play.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents with a variety of artifact-based effects like Sphere of Resistance, Null Rod, and Chalice of the Void, while also ramping up mana production quickly with cards like Mishra's Workshop, Ancient Tomb, and the Moxen. It utilizes powerful utility lands like Wasteland and Ghost Quarter to control the board while also having access to threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Karn, the Great Creator to quickly close out games. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive plays and efficient use of artifacts.

The deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to cheat big creatures into play while controlling the game with counterspells and disruption spells. It aims to quickly ramp into powerful threats like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Bolas's Citadel using Show and Tell and Tinker. The deck also includes card draw and tutoring to find key pieces, allowing for a strong control strategy that can quickly overwhelm opponents with its powerful creatures and game-changing spells.

This Vintage Azorius Tempo deck focuses on quickly playing powerful spells and disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining control of the board. The deck utilizes a combination of efficient creatures, counter magic, and removal spells to apply pressure and keep the opponent on the back foot. Key cards like Ancestral Recall, Swords to Plowshares, and Force of Will help the deck maintain card advantage and protect its threats. The deck's mana base is fine-tuned to support its powerful spells and ensure a smooth curve throughout the game.

This deck focuses on using control elements like counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent through efficient one-for-one interactions and powerful card draw spells. By controlling the board and manipulating resources effectively, it aims to outlast the opponent and ultimately win the game through attrition. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value by allowing the player to recur important cards from the graveyard throughout the game.

The deck focuses on assembling the Doomsday combo quickly and efficiently while protecting it with disruption spells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. Gitaxian Probe and Street Wraith provide card draw and filtering, while key cards like Ancestral Recall and Demonic Tutor help set up the combo. Thassa's Oracle serves as the win condition after resolving Doomsday, with options like Necropotence and Gush providing additional card advantage and digging for combo pieces. The deck aims to win the game in a single explosive turn by carefully planning out the sequence of spells to execute the Doomsday combo.
